_rationale: Locks the Decision A outcome (iter-3 of loop zRvo9CV23xZD, removal task lvGYODNAw8SP): `--enhanced` is not part of Go's CLI surface (cmd/lci/search.go `Flags:` block does not register it) so the Go binary rejects it as an unknown flag and exits 1. C++ used to implement `--enhanced` end-to-end (displayEnhancedResults port) but per karpathy rule 1 (Go is the bar) the flag was removed; the C++ binary now exits 1 with CLI11's unknown-argument message. This descriptor locks both binaries on the exit-code rejection path so any future re-introduction of `--enhanced` on either side breaks parity loudly.

ignore._rationale: stdout/stderr deliberately ignored via parse=exit-only: Go's urfave/cli emits a full help dump to stdout on unknown flags (`Incorrect Usage: flag provided but not defined: -enhanced` plus the entire `OPTIONS:` table) plus a `Fatal error:` line to stderr, while CLI11 (C++) emits a one-liner (`The following argument was not expected: --enhanced`) to stderr and nothing to stdout. Pinning either stream across two unrelated CLI libraries would over-specify and break on any upstream cosmetic tweak. The shared invariant — both reject the flag with exit 1 — is captured via expect_exit alone.

stable._rationale: Exit-only mode skips all text-canonicalization paths; expect_exit at the top level is the entirety of the parity contract for this descriptor.
